commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2bfb1f31fe80e6f528193713fcc75866e788dfda)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds optional `apiKeyRequired` metadata for skill versions, an async LLM evaluator/backfills, and API-key-required badges across marketplace browse, search, detail, and version UI.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect: source inspection of the latest head shows the new summary backfill has no cursor or continuation path. This is a feature PR rather than an end-user bug report, so no runtime reproduction was run.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blockers include contributor-supplied proof and maintainer acceptance of the classifier rollout, so this should stay in human review rather than a ClawSweeper repair lane.

target.title: feat: surface "API key required" skill attribute
target.body:
## Summary - **What changed:** Added an "API key required" attribute for every published skill version, computed asynchronously by an LLM analyser and surfaced as a small badge on cards, list rows, the detail header and the version panel. Designed cheap-first: deterministic short-circuits skip the LLM call whenever frontmatter declares a required env var, or when SKILL.md and the file manifest contain no sensitive keywords at all. - **Why:** Users browsing the marketplace currently have no way to know whether they need to provision an API key / secret before a skill will work. Surfacing this up-front sets expectations and avoids the "install → fail → debug env" loop. Doing the analysis async (post-publish) keeps publish latency unchanged and avoids forcing publishers to fill in yet another field. ## Linked Issue - Closes # - Related # ## Screenshots UI surfaces showing the new "API key required" badge: - [ ] Skill card (grid) — `SkillCard` - [ ] Skill list row — `SkillListItem` - [ ] Skill detail header — `SkillHeader` - [ ] Versions panel row — `SkillVersionsPanel` - [ ] Mobile / narrow view of the detail header > Attach captures of all four locations on a skill where `apiKeyRequired === true` and one where it is unset (badge absent), or `N/A`. ## Security / Trust Impact - [x] No security/trust impact - [ ] Security/trust impact explained The analyser only reads the SKILL.md content and the file path manifest that already live in the version doc. No secrets are sent to OpenAI, and the verdict is purely informational (`boolean | undefined`) — it does not gate publishing, downloads, or moderation. ## Data / Deploy Impact - [ ] No data/deploy impact - [x] Data/deploy impact explained **Schema (additive, all optional, no migration required):** | Table | Field | | ---------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | | `skillVersions` | `apiKeyRequired?: boolean` | | `skills.latestVersionSummary` | `apiKeyRequired?: boolean` (denormalised mirror) | | `skillSearchDigest.latestVersionSummary` | `apiKeyRequired?: boolean` (denormalised mirror) | **Backfill (manual, post-deploy, optional):** the catalogue will populate organically over time as new versions are published. To accelerate, run from the Convex CLI: ```bash # 1. Schedule the LLM analyser for every latest version that has never been analysed. bunx convex run llmEval:backfillApiKeyRequirement '{"dryRun": true}' # preview bunx convex run llmEval:backfillApiKeyRequirement '{"maxToSchedule": 50}' # incremental bunx convex run llmEval:backfillApiKeyRequirement # full sweep # Force a re-scan of everything (e.g. after a prompt change): bunx convex run llmEval:backfillApiKeyRequirement '{"onlyMissing": false}' # 2. Mirror existing version-level values onto `skills.latestVersionSummary` so legacy rows surface the badge. bunx convex run skills:backfillLatestVersionSummaryApiKeyRequiredInternal ``` Both backfills are idempotent and chunked (cursor + batchSize + delayMs); failures only affect the targeted version and leave `apiKeyRequired` unset (UI renders no badge). **OpenAI usage:** one extra `responses` API call per publish, only when the deterministic short-circuits do not fire. Gracefully degrades when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is unset (logs and exits). ## Verification - [x] `bun run ci:static` (oxfmt + oxlint clean across touched files) - [x] Focused tests for touched behavior: - [`convex/lib/parsedEnvSignals.test.ts`](convex/lib/parsedEnvSignals.test.ts) — frontmatter signal extraction across `clawdis` / `metadata.` / `frontmatter` shapes. - [`convex/lib/apiKeyRequirementPrompt.test.ts`](convex/lib/apiKeyRequirementPrompt.test.ts) — prompt assembly, response parsing, tri-state → boolean fold. - [`convex/llmEval.apiKey.test.ts`](convex/llmEval.apiKey.test.ts) — evaluator end-to-end: short-circuit A (frontmatter), short-circuit B (no keywords), LLM required / not_required / unknown, OpenAI error path, missing SKILL.md. - [`convex/llmEval.test.ts`](convex/llmEval.test.ts) — `backfillApiKeyRequirement` cursor / dryRun / maxToSchedule / onlyMissing branches + publish-hook source-grep guard. - [`convex/skills.apiKeyRequired.test.ts`](convex/skills.apiKeyRequired.test.ts) — `updateVersionApiKeyRequiredInternal` writes the version doc and mirrors onto `latestVersionSummary` only when the version is current latest. - [`convex/lib/skillPublish.test.ts`](convex/lib/skillPublish.test.ts) — publish flow still passes; new scheduler call is non-blocking. - [`src/components/ApiKeyRequiredBadge.test.tsx`](src/components/ApiKeyRequiredBadge.test.tsx) — renders only when strictly `true`. - [x] `bun run ci:unit` — 71/71 across the 7 test files above. - [ ] Broader gate when required (`ci:types-build`, `ci:packages`, `ci:e2e-http`, `ci:playwright-smoke`, `test:pw:local-auth`, `proof:ui`): - [ ] Other: manual smoke against `mongo-shell` (LLM verdict = required), `apieval-fixture-required` / `apieval-fixture-not-required` deterministic fixtures. ## Architecture notes (for reviewers) ```mermaid flowchart LR Publish[publishVersionForUser] -->|scheduler.runAfter 0| Eval[evaluateApiKeyRequirement
internalAction] Eval -->|frontmatter requires.env| W[updateVersionApiKeyRequiredInternal
true] Eval -->|no sensitive keywords| W2[updateVersionApiKeyRequiredInternal
false] Eval -->|OpenAI tri-state| W3[updateVersionApiKeyRequiredInternal
true / false / no-op] W & W2 & W3 -->|mirror when current latest| Summary[skills.latestVersionSummary] Summary --> UI[SkillCard / SkillListItem / SkillHeader / SkillVersionsPanel] ``` - Tri-state (`required` / `not_required` / `unknown`) folds into the schema's `boolean | undefined`. `unknown` deliberately leaves the field untouched so the UI degrades to "no badge" rather than fabricating a verdict. - The denormalised mirror on `latestVersionSummary` is the canonical source for list / search surfaces; the live version doc is only read on the detail page. - Search paths intentionally return `version: null`; `useSkillsBrowseModel` synthesises a minimal `latestVersion` stub from `entry.apiKeyRequired` so the badge still renders without an extra round-trip. ## Out of scope - Publisher-facing UI to override the verdict (could be added later as an `apiKeyRequiredOverride` field). - Surfacing the analyser's `rationale` / `envVars` in the UI; today they are only logged. - Re-running the analyser when SKILL.md is edited without a version bump (versions are immutable, so this is moot in the current data model).

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
